# AsyncExitStack backport for Python 3.5+

- Free software: PSF license
- Documentation: <https://docs.python.org/3.7/library/contextlib.html#contextlib.AsyncExitStack>

This package contains code from the CPython project.

## Install

```
pip install async_exit_stack
```

## Usage example

```python
from async_exit_stack import AsyncExitStack

async def some_async_function():
    async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
        connections = [await stack.enter_async_context(get_connection())
            for i in range(5)]
        # All opened connections will automatically be released at the end of
        # the async with statement, even if attempts to open a connection
        # later in the list raise an exception.
```

## Reference

Refer to [Python 3.7 contextlib documentation](https://docs.python.org/3.7/library/contextlib.html#contextlib.AsyncExitStack)
for a complete reference and more context.

{python}`class AsyncExitStack`

: An asynchronous context manager, similar to ExitStack, that supports combining
  both synchronous and asynchronous context managers, as well as having
  coroutines for cleanup logic.

  The close() method is not implemented, aclose() must be used instead.

  {python}`enter_async_context(cm)`

  : Similar to enter_context() but expects an asynchronous context manager.

  {python}`push_async_exit(exit)`

  : Similar to push() but expects either an asynchronous context manager or a coroutine.

  {python}`push_async_callback(callback, *args, **kwds)`

  : Similar to callback() but expects a coroutine.

  {python}`aclose()`

  : Similar to close() but properly handles awaitables.

# History

## 1.0.0 (2018-05-07)

- First release based on Python 3.7b4.

classes.name: async_exit_stack._async_exit_stack.AsyncExitStack

methods.name: async_exit_stack._async_exit_stack.AsyncExitStack.pop_all
documentation: Preserve the context stack by transferring it to a new instance.

methods.name: async_exit_stack._async_exit_stack.AsyncExitStack.push
documentation:
Registers a callback with the standard __exit__ method signature.
Can suppress exceptions the same way __exit__ method can.
Also accepts any object with an __exit__ method (registering a call
to the method instead of the object itself).

methods.name: async_exit_stack._async_exit_stack.AsyncExitStack.enter_context
documentation:
Enters the supplied context manager.
If successful, also pushes its __exit__ method as a callback and
returns the result of the __enter__ method.

methods.name: async_exit_stack._async_exit_stack.AsyncExitStack.callback
documentation:
Registers an arbitrary callback and arguments.
Cannot suppress exceptions.

methods.name: async_exit_stack._async_exit_stack.AsyncExitStack._push_cm_exit
documentation: Helper to correctly register callbacks to __exit__ methods.

methods.name: async_exit_stack._async_exit_stack.AsyncExitStack.enter_async_context
documentation:
Enters the supplied async context manager.
If successful, also pushes its __aexit__ method as a callback and
returns the result of the __aenter__ method.

methods.name: async_exit_stack._async_exit_stack.AsyncExitStack.push_async_exit
documentation:
Registers a coroutine function with the standard __aexit__ method
signature.
Can suppress exceptions the same way __aexit__ method can.
Also accepts any object with an __aexit__ method (registering a call
to the method instead of the object itself).

methods.name: async_exit_stack._async_exit_stack.AsyncExitStack.push_async_callback
documentation:
Registers an arbitrary coroutine function and arguments.
Cannot suppress exceptions.

methods.name: async_exit_stack._async_exit_stack.AsyncExitStack._push_async_cm_exit
documentation:
Helper to correctly register coroutine function to __aexit__
method.

Async context manager for dynamic management of a stack of exit
callbacks.
For example:
    async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
        connections = [await stack.enter_async_context(get_connection())
            for i in range(5)]
        # All opened connections will automatically be released at the
        # end of the async with statement, even if attempts to open a
        # connection later in the list raise an exception.
